volumeTitle: Birds. Lysistrata. Women at the Thesmophoria
label: Birds (Section 274 - Section 354)

sections.label: Section 274
sections.translation: Hi ! I say ! You !

sections.label: Section 274b
sections.translation: What are you shouting for ?

sections.label: Section 274c
sections.translation: Why , here ' s another bird .

sections.label: Section 275
sections.original: νὴ Δίʼ ἕτερος δῆτα χοὖτος ἔξεδρον χρόαν ἔχων .
sections.translation: Aye , indeed ; this one ' s a foreign bird too . To Epops . What is this bird from beyond the mountains with a look as solemn as it is stupid ?

sections.label: Section 277
sections.translation: He is called the Mede .

sections.label: Section 277b
sections.translation: The Mede ! But , by Heracles , how , if a Mede , has he flown here without a camel ?

sections.label: Section 279
sections.original: ἕτερος αὖ λόφον κατειληφώς τις ὄρνις οὑτοσί .
sections.translation: Here ' s another bird with a crest .

sections.label: Section 280
sections.original: τί τὸ τέρας τουτί ποτʼ ἐστίν ; οὐ σὺ μόνος ἄρʼ ἦσθʼ ἔποψ ,
sections.translation: Ah ! that ' s curious . I say , Epops , you are not the only one of your kind then ?

sections.label: Section 281b
sections.translation: This bird is the son of Philocles , who is the son of Epops ; so that , you see , I am his grandfather ; just as one might say , Hipponicus , the son of Callias , who is the son of Hipponicus .

sections.label: Section 284
sections.original: Καλλίας ἄρʼ οὗτος οὕρνις ἐστίν · ὡς πτερορρυεῖ .
sections.translation: Then this bird is Callias ! Why , what a lot of his feathers he has lost !

sections.label: Section 285
sections.original: ἅτε γὰρ ὢν γενναῖος ὑπό τε συκοφαντῶν τίλλεται ,
sections.translation: That ' s because he is honest ; so the informers set upon him and the women too pluck out his feathers .

sections.label: Section 287
sections.original: ὦ Πόσειδον ἕτερος αὖ τις βαπτὸς ὄρνις οὑτοσί .
sections.translation: By Poseidon , do you see that many-coloured bird ? What is his name ?

sections.label: Section 288b
sections.translation: This one ? That ' s the glutton .

sections.label: Section 289
sections.original: ἔστι γὰρ κατωφαγᾶς τις ἄλλος ἢ Κλεώνυμος ;
sections.translation: Is there another glutton besides Cleonymus ?

sections.label: Section 290
sections.original: πῶς ἂν οὖν Κλεώνυμός γʼ ὢν οὐκ ἀπέβαλε τὸν λόφον ;
sections.translation: But why , if he is Cleonymus , has he not thrown away his crest ?

sections.label: Section 291
sections.original: ἀλλὰ μέντοι τίς ποθʼ ἡ λόφωσις ἡ τῶν ὀρνέων ;
sections.translation: But what is the meaning of all these crests ? Have these birds come to contend for the double stadium prize ?

sections.label: Section 292b
sections.translation: They are like the Carians , who cling to the crests of their mountains for greater safety .

sections.label: Section 294
sections.original: ὦ Πόσειδον οὐχ ὁρᾷς ὅσον συνείλεκται κακὸν
sections.translation: Oh , Poseidon ! look what awful swarms of birds are gathering here !

sections.label: Section 295b
sections.original: ὦναξ Ἄπολλον τοῦ νέφους . ἰοὺ ἰού ,
sections.translation: By Phoebus ! what a cloud ! The entrance to the stage is no longer visible so closely do they fly together .

sections.label: Section 297
sections.original: οὑτοσὶ πέρδιξ , ἐκεινοσί γε νὴ Δίʼ ἀτταγᾶς ,
sections.translation: Here is the partridge .

sections.label: Section 298
sections.original: οὑτοσὶ δὲ πηνέλοψ , ἐκεινηὶ δέ γʼ ἀλκυών .
sections.translation: There is the poachard .

sections.label: Section 299
sections.translation: To Epops . What ' s that bird behind the kingfisher ?

sections.label: Section 299b
sections.translation: That ' s the barber .

sections.label: Section 300
sections.translation: What ? a bird a barber ?

sections.label: Section 300b
sections.translation: Why , Sporgilus is one .

sections.label: Section 301
sections.translation: Here comes the owl .

sections.label: Section 301b
sections.original: τί φῄς ; τίς γλαῦκʼ Ἀθήναζʼ ἤγαγεν ;
sections.translation: And who is it brings an owl to Athens ?

sections.label: Section 302
sections.original: κίττα τρυγὼν κορυδὸς ἐλεᾶς ὑποθυμὶς περιστερὰ
sections.translation: Here is the magpie , the turtle-dove , the swallow , the horned-owl , the buzzard , the pigeon , the falcon , the ring-dove , the cuckoo , the red-foot , the red-cap , the purple-cap , the kestrel , the diver , the ousel , the osprey , the woodpecker . . .

sections.label: Section 305
sections.original: ἰοὺ ἰοὺ τῶν ὀρνέων , ἰοὺ ἰοὺ τῶν κοψίχων ·
sections.translation: Oh ! what a lot of birds ! Oh ! what a lot of blackbirds ! How they scold , how they come rushing up ! What a noise ! what a noise ! Can they be bearing us ill-will ? Oh ! there ! there ! they are opening their beaks and staring at us .

sections.label: Section 308b
sections.translation: Why , so they are .

sections.label: Section 310
sections.translation: Popopopopopo . Where is he who called me ? Where am I to find him ?

sections.label: Section 312
sections.original: οὑτοσὶ πάλαι πάρειμι κοὐκ ἀποστατῶ φίλων .
sections.translation: I have been waiting for you a long while ! I never fail in my word to my friends .

sections.label: Section 313
sections.original: τί τί τί τί τί τί τί τί · τίνα λόγον ἄρα ποτὲ
sections.translation: Tititititititi . What good news

sections.label: Section 315
sections.translation: have you for me ?

sections.label: Section 316
sections.translation: Something that concerns our common safety , and that is just as pleasant as it is to the point . Two men , who are subtle reasoners , have come here to seek me .

sections.label: Section 318
sections.translation: Where ? How ? What are you saying ?

sections.label: Section 320
sections.original: φήμʼ ἀπʼ ἀνθρώπων ἀφῖχθαι δεῦρο πρεσβύτα δύο ·
sections.translation: I say , two old men have come from the abode of humans to propose a vast and splendid scheme to us .

sections.label: Section 322
sections.original: ὦ μέγιστον ἐξαμαρτὼν ἐξ ὅτου ʼτράφην ἐγώ ,
sections.translation: Oh ! it ' s a horrible , unheard-of crime ! What are you saying ?

sections.label: Section 323b
sections.translation: Never let my words scare you .

sections.label: Section 323c
sections.translation: What have you done to me ?

sections.label: Section 324
sections.original: ἄνδρʼ ἐδεξάμην ἐραστὰ τῆσδε τῆς ξυνουσίας .
sections.translation: I have welcomed two men , who wish to live with us .

sections.label: Section 325
sections.translation: And you have dared to do that !

sections.label: Section 325b
sections.translation: Yes , and I am delighted at having done so .

sections.label: Section 326
sections.translation: And are they already with us ?

sections.label: Section 326b
sections.translation: Just as much as I am .

sections.label: Section 1.327
sections.translation: Ah ! ah ! we are betrayed ; ' tis sacrilege ! Our friend ,

sections.label: Section 1.330
sections.translation: he who picked up corn-seeds in the same plains as ourselves , has violated our ancient laws ; he has broken the oaths that bind all birds ; he has laid a snare for me , he has handed us over to the attacks of that impious race which , throughout all time ,

sections.label: Section 1.335
sections.translation: has never ceased to war against us .

sections.label: Section 336
sections.original: ἀλλὰ πρὸς τοῦτον μὲν ἡμῖν ἐστιν ὕστερος λόγος ·
sections.translation: As for this traitorous bird , we will decide his case later , but the two old men shall be punished forthwith ; we are going to tear them to pieces .

sections.label: Section 338b
sections.translation: It ' s all over with us .

sections.label: Section 339
sections.original: αἴτιος μέντοι σὺ νῷν εἶ τῶν κακῶν τούτων μόνος .
sections.translation: You are the sole cause of all our trouble .

sections.label: Section 340
sections.original: ἐπὶ τί γάρ μʼ ἐκεῖθεν ἦγες ;
sections.translation: Why did you bring me from down yonder ?

sections.label: Section 340b
sections.translation: To have you with me .

sections.label: Section 341
sections.translation: Say rather to have me melt into tears .

sections.label: Section 341b
sections.translation: Go on ! you are talking nonsense . How will you weep with your eyes pecked out ?

sections.label: Section 343
sections.translation: Io ! io ! forward to the attack , throw yourselves upon the foe ,

sections.label: Section 345
sections.translation: spill his blood ; take to your wings and surround them on all sides . Woe to them ! let us get to work with our beaks , let us devour them .

sections.label: Section 350
sections.original: οὔτε πολιὸν πέλαγος ἔστιν ὅ τι δέξεται
sections.translation: Nothing can save them from our wrath , neither the mountain forests , nor the clouds that float in the sky , nor the foaming deep .

sections.label: Section 352
sections.original: ἀλλὰ μὴ μέλλωμεν ἤδη τώδε τίλλειν καὶ δάκνειν .
sections.translation: Come , peck , tear to ribbons . Where is the chief of the cohort ? Let him engage the right wing .

sections.label: Section 354
sections.translation: This is the fatal moment . Where shall I fly to , unfortunate wretch that I am ?
